name: Order Book
summary: Retrieves the user's order book (all orders placed for the trading day) via a POST call. The request sends a jData JSON object (uid required, optional prd) plus the login jKey, and on success returns a JSON array of order objects with full per-order detail (symbol, price, qty, status, validity, etc.).

request_params.name: jData*
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list.

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: Order book success or failure indication.

response_fields.name: exch
response_fields.description: Exchange Segment.

response_fields.name: tsym
response_fields.description: Trading symbol / contract on which order is placed.

response_fields.name: norenordno
response_fields.description: Noren Order Number.

response_fields.name: prc*
response_fields.description: Order Price [If prc is junk value other than numbers] "Order price cannot be zero".

response_fields.name: qty*
response_fields.description: Order Quantity [If qty is junk value other than numbers].

response_fields.name: prd
response_fields.description: Display product alias name, using prarr returned in user details.

response_fields.name: status
response_fields.description: Order status.

response_fields.name: trantype*
response_fields.description: B -> BUY, S -> SELL [transtype should be 'B' or 'S' else reject].

response_fields.name: prctyp
response_fields.description: Price type.

response_fields.name: fillshares
response_fields.description: Total Traded Quantity of this order.

response_fields.name: avgprc
response_fields.description: Average trade price of total traded quantity.

response_fields.name: rejreason
response_fields.description: If order is rejected, reason in text form.

response_fields.name: exchordid
response_fields.description: Exchange Order Number.

response_fields.name: cancelqty
response_fields.description: Canceled quantity for order which is in status cancelled.

response_fields.name: remarks
response_fields.description: Any message Entered during order entry.

response_fields.name: dscqty*
response_fields.description: Order disclosed quantity [If dscqty is junk value other than numbers].

response_fields.name: trgprc
response_fields.description: Order trigger price.

response_fields.name: ret*
response_fields.description: Order validity [ret should be DAY / EOS / IOC else reject].

response_fields.name: bpprc
response_fields.description: Book Profit Price applicable only if product is selected as B (Bracket order).

response_fields.name: blprc
response_fields.description: Book loss Price applicable only if product is selected as H and B (High Leverage and Bracket order).

response_fields.name: trailprc
response_fields.description: Trailing Price applicable only if product is selected as H and B (High Leverage and Bracket order).

response_fields.name: amo*
response_fields.description: The message "Invalid AMO" will be displayed if the "amo" field is not sent with a "Yes" value. If amo is not required, do not send this field.

response_fields.name: pp
response_fields.description: Price precision.

response_fields.name: ti
response_fields.description: Tick size.

response_fields.name: ls
response_fields.description: Lot size.

response_fields.name: token
response_fields.description: Contract Token.

response_fields.name: snoordt
response_fields.description: 0 for profit leg and 1 for stoploss leg.

response_fields.name: snonum
response_fields.description: This field will be present for product H and B; and only if it is profit/sl order.

response_fields.name: dname
response_fields.description: Broker specific contract display name.

response_fields.name: rorgqty
response_fields.description: To be used in get margin from modify window.

response_fields.name: rorgprc
response_fields.description: To be used in get margin from modify window.

response_fields.name: orgtrgprc
response_fields.description: To be used in get margin from modify window, for H/B products only.

response_fields.name: orgblprc
response_fields.description: To be used in get margin from modify window, for H/B products only.

response_fields.name: algo_name
response_fields.description: Algo Name.

notes: Two response shapes documented: on success the body is a JSON ARRAY of order objects (not a single object); on failure it is a single JSON object with stat=Not_Ok plus request_time and emsg (Error message). The curl example uses a placeholder host 'https://BaseURL/OrderBook'; the real production URL is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI/OrderBook. The source curl has a stray smart-quote typo: }“&jKey= — the } should be followed by a normal straight-quote ' closing the --data string before &jKey (reproduced verbatim above). The request body has only two fields: uid (mandatory, shown as uid*) and prd (optional, possible values H / M / ...). Several response fields' mandatory asterisks (prc*, qty*, trantype*, dscqty*, ret*, amo*) are carried over from the order-placement field table; in this read-only order-book response they indicate fields normally present rather than caller-supplied requirements. Conditional response fields: bpprc only when product=B (Bracket); blprc/trailprc only when product=H or B (High Leverage / Bracket); snonum and orgtrgprc/orgblprc only for H/B products. amo: an "Invalid AMO" message results if 'Yes' is not sent — omit the field entirely when AMO is not required. The trailing row 'C / CUST_FIRM_C' at the top of page 20 is an isolated key/value pair with no description; its role (a literal field named 'C') is ambiguous but transcribed as shown. The sample success JSON uses curly/smart quotes (“ ”) rather than straight ASCII quotes, and the second object is missing a comma after the rejreason line (both reproduced verbatim from the source).
issues: The success sample (page 18) and the response-field continuation (pages 19-20) reuse the same generic 'Possible value / Json Fields / Description' three-column layout used by the preceding Single Order / Place Order tables, so a few possible-value cells (e.g. prctyp 'LMT', trantype 'B / S', ret 'DAY / EOS / IOC', amo 'Yes') are inherited enum hints rather than exhaustive lists. The fields uid, actid, orddttm, ordenttm and extm appear in the response table with a name but no description cell. The trailing 'C / CUST_FIRM_C' row is an isolated key/value pair with no description. The mandatory asterisks on response fields are unusual for a read-only book response and are reproduced from the image.
